answer:
I now have a complete picture. Here's the answer:

## What you can safely assume on `Context` in both Cloudflare Workers and Node

The `Context` class is defined in `src/context.ts:293`. It's built on Web Standards (per **ADR-01**, which mandates that core code uses only WHATWG primitives — `Request`, `Response`, `Headers`, `URL`, `fetch`, `ReadableStream`, etc. — with no runtime-specific globals). That guarantee is what makes the cross-runtime story work.

### ✅ Always available (truly portable)

These are unconditional on every runtime:

| Property | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `c.req` | `HonoRequest` | Lazy-instantiated wrapper around the Web `Request`. Always safe. |
| `c.res` | `Response` | Lazy-created if you read it; setting it marks `finalized = true`. |
| `c.env` | `E['Bindings']` | **Defaults to `{}`** (`src/context.ts:315`). See caveat below. |
| `c.error` | `Error \| undefined` | Set by middleware after a thrown handler. |
| `c.finalized` | `boolean` | |
| `c.render` / `c.setRenderer` / `c.setLayout` / `c.getLayout` | | Pure JS, no runtime deps. |
| Helpers like `c.json()`, `c.text()`, `c.html()`, `c.body()`, `c.redirect()`, `c.header()`, `c.status()`, `c.set()`, `c.get()`, `c.var` | | All built on Web Standards. |

### ⚠️ Conditional — these will throw or be empty on Node

**`c.executionCtx`** — `src/context.ts:391` throws `"This context has no ExecutionContext"` if not provided by the adapter. Cloudflare Workers (workerd) populates this; `@hono/node-server` typically does **not**. Guard with try/catch or check before use.

**`c.event`** — `src/context.ts:378` throws `"This context has no FetchEvent"` unless you're on a service-worker–style adapter (Cloudflare's old service-worker mode, `src/adapter/service-worker`). **Don't use this** if you want Node compatibility.

**`c.env`** — this is the big footgun. On **workerd**, `c.env` is the bindings object (KV, D1, secrets, etc.) injected by the runtime. On **Node**, `c.env` defaults to `{}` because Node has no concept of bindings — env vars live on `process.env`. The codebase has a dedicated helper for this exact problem:

```ts
import { env } from 'hono/adapter'
// src/helper/adapter/index.ts:10
const { MY_VAR } = env<{ MY_VAR: string }>(c)
```

That helper detects the runtime via `getRuntimeKey()` and pulls from `process.env` on Node/Bun/edge-light, `Deno.env` on Deno, and `c.env` on workerd. **Use it instead of `c.env` directly** if you want a single handler that works on both.

### Recommendations for your handler

1. Touch only `c.req`, `c.res`, `c.json/text/html/...`, `c.header/status`, `c.set/get/var`, `c.error`, `c.render` — these are unconditionally portable.
2. Use `env(c)` from `hono/adapter` instead of `c.env` for environment values.
3. Avoid `c.event` entirely (service-worker only).
4. Treat `c.executionCtx` as optional — guard it, or only use it on code paths gated to Cloudflare. There's no portable equivalent for `waitUntil`/`passThroughOnException` on Node.
5. If you need to branch behavior, `getRuntimeKey()` from `hono/adapter` returns `'node' | 'workerd' | 'bun' | 'deno' | 'fastly' | 'edge-light' | 'other'`.

Note that `@hono/node-server` lives in a separate package (per ADR-01, to keep Node types out of core), so the Node runtime support isn't visible in this repo — but the contract above is what core guarantees the adapter must uphold.

1	/**
     2	 * @module
     3	 * Adapter Helper for Hono.
     4	 */
     5	
     6	import type { Context } from '../../context'
     7	
     8	export type Runtime = 'node' | 'deno' | 'bun' | 'workerd' | 'fastly' | 'edge-light' | 'other'
     9	
    10	export const env = <
    11	  T extends Record<string, unknown>,
    12	  C extends Context = Context<{
    13	    Bindings: T
    14	  }>,
    15	>(
    16	  c: T extends Record<string, unknown> ? Context : C,
    17	  runtime?: Runtime
    18	): T & C['env'] => {
    19	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    20	  const global = globalThis as any
    21	  const globalEnv = global?.process?.env as T
    22	
    23	  runtime ??= getRuntimeKey()
    24	
    25	  const runtimeEnvHandlers: Record<string, () => T> = {
    26	    bun: () => globalEnv,
    27	    node: () => globalEnv,
    28	    'edge-light': () => globalEnv,
    29	    deno: () => {
    30	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment
    31	      // @ts-ignore
    32	      return Deno.env.toObject() as T
    33	    },
    34	    workerd: () => c.env,
    35	    // On Fastly Compute, you can use the ConfigStore to manage user-defined data.
    36	    fastly: () => ({}) as T,
    37	    other: () => ({}) as T,
    38	  }
    39	
    40	  return runtimeEnvHandlers[runtime]()
    41	}
    42	
    43	export const knownUserAgents: Partial<Record<Runtime, string>> = {
    44	  deno: 'Deno',
    45	  bun: 'Bun',
    46	  workerd: 'Cloudflare-Workers',
    47	  node: 'Node.js',
    48	}
    49	
    50	export const getRuntimeKey = (): Runtime => {
    51	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    52	  const global = globalThis as any
    53	
    54	  // check if the current runtime supports navigator.userAgent
    55	  const userAgentSupported =
    56	    typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && typeof navigator.userAgent === 'string'
    57	
    58	  // if supported, check the user agent
    59	  if (userAgentSupported) {
    60	    for (const [runtimeKey, userAgent] of Object.entries(knownUserAgents)) {
    61	      if (checkUserAgentEquals(userAgent)) {
    62	        return runtimeKey as Runtime
    63	      }
    64	    }
    65	  }
    66	
    67	  // check if running on Edge Runtime
    68	  if (typeof global?.EdgeRuntime === 'string') {
    69	    return 'edge-light'
    70	  }
    71	
    72	  // check if running on Fastly
    73	  if (global?.fastly !== undefined) {
    74	    return 'fastly'
    75	  }
    76	
    77	  // userAgent isn't supported before Node v21.1.0; so fallback to the old way
    78	  if (global?.process?.release?.name === 'node') {
    79	    return 'node'
    80	  }
    81	
    82	  // couldn't detect the runtime
    83	  return 'other'
    84	}
    85	
    86	export const checkUserAgentEquals = (platform: string): boolean => {
    87	  const userAgent = navigator.userAgent
    88	
    89	  return userAgent.startsWith(platform)
    90	}
